rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LIGHTFOOT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Omtion For Judgment On The Pleadings Against Pltf
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 29, 2011, Line 1. DEFENDANT PENINSULA CORRIDOR JOINT POWERS BOARD (CALTRAIN), NATIONAL RAILROAD PASSENGER CORPORATION (AMTRAK) Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. The motion is off calendar because plaintiff filed an amended complaint on December 19, 2011. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GHIGLIOTTI J. JEROME JR VS. VANESSA LINDSAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 29, 2011, Line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT JEROME GHIGLOTTI, JEROME J. GHIGLOTTI JR., MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. Denied. Cross-Defendants dispute the facts in the Cross-Complaint, but have not shown a basis to strike any matter asserted in the pleading pursuant to CCP §§435-437. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GHIGLIOTTI J. JEROME JR VS. VANESSA LINDSAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 29, 2011, Line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT JEROME GHIGLOTTI JR., J., JEROME GHIGLOTTI DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. Cross-Complainant has pled facts sufficient to constitute causes of action and the pleadings are not uncertain. The Cross-Complaint was timely filed at the same time as the answer. CCP §§428.10 and 428.50(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE BERNAL VS. NIBBI INVESTMENTS, A BUSINESS WHOSE FORM IS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Of Pltf'S Complaint And To Set Aside The Default, And Default Judgment If Any Taken Against Her On The X-Complaint In This Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 29, 2011, Line 6. PLAINTIFF MICHELLE BERNAL Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Of Plaintiff'S Complaint And To Set Aside The Default, And Default Judgment If Any Taken Against Her On The Cross-Complaint In This Action. Granted. Plaintiff has shown she is entitled to relief under CCP § 473(b). Plaintiff must pay the balance due on the initial filing fee. Request for sanctions and attorneys fee denied.
